title: Israeli Airstrikes Kill Seven in Southern Lebanon Despite Active Ceasefire With Hezbollah
summary: Israeli forces conducted approximately 50 airstrikes over 24 hours in southern Lebanon, killing at least seven people in multiple villages despite a ceasefire in effect since April 17.
contents:
## Israeli Strikes Continue in Southern Lebanon

### Casualty Report
Israeli airstrikes on southern Lebanon killed at least seven people and wounded others on Saturday, May 2, despite a ceasefire between Israel and Hezbollah that took effect on April 17, 2026. Lebanon's state-run National News Agency documented strikes across multiple villages.

### Strike Details
An airstrike on a car in the village of Kfar Dajal killed two people. Another hit struck a home in Lwaizeh, killing three. Two additional fatalities occurred in a strike on the village of Shoukin. Israel's military Arabic-language spokeswoman Lt. Col. Ella Waweya reported approximately 50 Israeli airstrikes over the preceding 24 hours targeting "Hezbollah infrastructure and members."

### Forced Displacement Orders
Israel's military issued new evacuation warnings for residents of nine southern Lebanese villages on Saturday. Lebanon's Parliament Speaker Nabih Berri accused Israel of "using the ceasefire as cover to intensify attacks." Friday's strikes killed 12 people, including casualties in a town where Israel's army had issued forced displacement orders despite the ceasefire.

### Hezbollah Response
Hezbollah claimed a drone attack on Israeli troops gathered inside a house in the coastal village of Bayed. The group maintained operations despite the ceasefire framework. The broader Lebanon Ministry of Public Health tracked mounting casualties as the ceasefire appeared to hold in name while violence continued on the ground. Five IDF divisions (98th, 36th, 162nd, 91st, 146th, 210th) maintained positions in southern Lebanon.
